summary: The team held a pre-launch alignment call for the Comply v2 GA deployment scheduled for April 7th. Ravi confirmed infrastructure dry-runs in staging went cleanly, with a minor Lambda cold-start issue to be addressed via a warm-up ping. Nina reported full regression suite passage across all four compliance frameworks and three tenant sizes, flagging a p99 latency concern at 50 concurrent requests that will be monitored via a new MetricFlow alert. The team confirmed a phased rollout plan, a clean feature-flag-based rollback strategy, and agreed to keep the v1 reporting service running in parallel for two weeks post-GA.
actionItems: Ravi Gupta: Add Lambda warm-up ping to the pre-deploy checklist before the April 6th deployment
Ravi Gupta and Nina Kowalski: Coordinate and configure MetricFlow queue depth alert for the reporting service by April 6th
Ananya Sharma: Update the deployment runbook with alert config and warm-up ping notes and circulate for final review by EOD Thursday
Mike Romano: Track PCI DSS hardcoded version strings cleanup as a v2.0.1 ticket post-launch

keyMoments.text: Nina flagged p99 latency of 22 seconds at 50 concurrent requests, exceeding the 15-second SLO target during load testing
keyMoments.speaker: Nina Kowalski

keyMoments.text: Ravi confirmed staging dry-run was clean and infrastructure is ready, with the Lambda cold-start warm-up as the only minor issue identified
keyMoments.speaker: Ravi Gupta

keyMoments.text: Ravi detailed the resilience architecture including redundant processing nodes and circuit breaker pattern, directly informed by the March Detect outage
keyMoments.speaker: Ravi Gupta

keyMoments.text: Rollback plan confirmed as instant via feature flag with v1 service running in parallel for two weeks post-GA, giving the team full safety net
keyMoments.speaker: Ravi Gupta
